articles.title: Meta is reentering the AI race with a new model called Muse Spark
articles.summary: Meta Superintelligence Labs launched Muse Spark, its first model after a major AI overhaul. The model powers Meta AI and will integrate across WhatsApp, Instagram, Facebook, Messenger, and smart glasses.

articles.title: The vibes are off at OpenAI
articles.summary: Despite raising $122 billion and planning an IPO, OpenAI faces instability concerns from executive departures and discontinued projects, threatening its market leadership.

articles.title: Unionized ProPublica staff are on strike over AI, layoffs, and wages
articles.summary: ProPublica's unionized staff staged a 24-hour strike over unresolved collective bargaining issues including AI protections, job security, and wages. The union has been negotiating since 2023.

articles.title: Astropad’s Workbench reimagines remote desktop for AI agents, not IT support
articles.summary: Astropad's Workbench enables remote monitoring and control of AI agents on Mac Minis via iPhone or iPad with low-latency streaming, reimagining remote desktop technology for AI workflows.

articles.title: OpenAI releases a new safety blueprint to address the rise in child sexual exploitation
articles.summary: OpenAI released a Child Safety Blueprint to combat rising child sexual exploitation connected to AI advancements. The initiative addresses safety concerns in AI development.

articles.title: Databricks co-founder wins prestigious ACM award, says ‘AGI is here already’
articles.summary: Databricks co-founder Matei Zaharia won the prestigious ACM award and claims AGI already exists, arguing it's misunderstood. He's now focusing on AI applications for research.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, secured $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native infrastructure challenging AWS and Google Cloud.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code charges $20-$200/month for AI coding assistance, but open-source alternative Goose offers identical functionality for free, running locally without subscription fees or usage limits.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B after a viral billboard hiring campaign featuring AI tokens and coding challenges. The creative recruitment stunt helped the startup attract top engineering talent in a competitive market.

articles.title: Thousands of consumer routers hacked by Russia's military
articles.summary: Russia's military intelligence agency hacked 18,000-40,000 consumer routers globally to redirect users to credential-stealing sites for espionage operations.

articles.title: OpenClaw gives users yet another reason to be freaked out about security
articles.summary: OpenClaw, a popular AI agentic tool with 347,000 GitHub stars, poses security risks due to its broad system access permissions. A recently patched vulnerability highlights dangers of granting such extensive access to autonomous AI agents.

articles.title: New Rowhammer attacks give complete control of machines running Nvidia GPUs
articles.summary: Researchers discovered three novel Rowhammer attacks exploiting memory bit flips in Nvidia GPUs, allowing malicious cloud users to gain complete root control of shared host machines.

articles.title: Mustafa Suleyman: AI development won’t hit a wall anytime soon—here’s why
articles.summary: Mustafa Suleyman argues AI development won't plateau soon, citing exponential growth trends that humans struggle to grasp intuitively. Training data for frontier AI models has increased 1 trillion times since 2010.

articles.title: The Download: water threats in Iran and AI’s impact on what entrepreneurs make
articles.summary: Desalination plants in Iran face vulnerability amid escalating conflict, with threats to destroy them potentially devastating water supply across the Middle East. The crisis could severely impact farming, industry, and drinking water access in the region.

articles.title: Desalination plants in the Middle East are increasingly vulnerable
articles.summary: Middle East desalination plants face escalating vulnerability amid regional conflicts. Iran accused the US of attacking a facility, disrupting water supply to villages, highlighting critical infrastructure risks.

articles.title: ALTK‑Evolve: On‑the‑Job Learning for AI Agents
articles.summary: ALTK-Evolve enables AI agents to learn and improve directly during task execution. The system allows agents to adapt their behavior based on real-world feedback without retraining.

articles.title: Safetensors is Joining the PyTorch Foundation
articles.summary: Safetensors, a safe file format for storing machine learning models, is joining the PyTorch Foundation. This move strengthens the format's adoption across the ML community.

articles.title: Welcome Gemma 4: Frontier multimodal intelligence on device
articles.summary: Google introduces Gemma 4, a frontier multimodal AI model designed for on-device processing. The model enables advanced intelligence capabilities while maintaining privacy through local deployment.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future of AI development and its potential impact. The conversation explores emerging trends and next steps in artificial intelligence advancement.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels are leveraging Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and boost creative capabilities for content creation and business operations.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft released an open-source toolkit for sustainable agriculture, enabling farmers to leverage AI and technology for improved crop management and environmental practices.

articles.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
articles.summary: Google introduces two new inference tiers for Gemini API—Flex and Priority—to balance cost and latency for developers.

articles.title: Create, edit and share videos at no cost in Google Vids
articles.summary: Google Vids introduces free AI-powered video creation and editing using Lyria 3 and Veo 3.1 for high-quality video generation without cost.

articles.title: We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
articles.summary: Google partnered with the Brazilian government to create a satellite imagery map designed to protect and monitor Brazil's forests more effectively.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google released Gemma 4, its most capable open-source AI models designed for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows, offering improved intelligence compared to previous versions.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
articles.summary: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live introduces an improved voice model with enhanced precision and reduced latency for more natural, fluid voice interactions.

articles.title: Protecting people from harmful manipulation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind researches AI manipulation risks in finance and health sectors, implementing new safety measures to protect people from harmful AI manipulation.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family featuring mixture-of-experts architecture.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia unveiled DLSS 5, a generative AI-powered graphics technology that enhances video game photorealism in real-time up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting conditions.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 covers major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR pricing, Cursor's coding automations, and new interactive visualization features from ChatGPT and Anthropic.

articles.title: AI’s software development success and central management needs
articles.summary: AI has entered early production for many enterprises, but adoption is outpacing governance and integration. Companies must establish stronger controls and integrate AI into existing platforms.

articles.title: Microsoft open-source toolkit secures AI agents at runtime
articles.summary: Microsoft released an open-source toolkit for runtime security of AI agents in enterprises. It addresses risks from autonomous language models executing code faster than traditional policy controls can manage.

articles.title: Asylon and Thrive Logic bring physical AI to enterprise perimeter security
articles.summary: Asylon and Thrive Logic partner to deploy physical AI robots with autonomous patrols and AI analytics for enterprise perimeter security, enabling real-time incident response and reducing security response delays.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to identify which agents cause task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, addressing debugging challenges in collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses localization, self-localization, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As large language models become commoditized, sophisticated engineering harnesses around them are emerging as the true competitive advantage, as revealed by Anthropic's Claude Code leak.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware exploits macOS binaries to steal credentials from developers running local AI agents on Mac Minis, targeting the growing OpenClaw AI agent ecosystem.

articles.title: Why Meta’s V-JEPA 2.1 model is a massive step forward for real-world AI
articles.summary: Meta's V-JEPA 2.1 advances video self-supervised learning by effectively balancing motion tracking with spatial detail, overcoming a longstanding challenge in AI model development.

articles.title: Z.AI Introduces GLM-5.1: An Open-Weight 754B Agentic Model That Achieves SOTA on SWE-Bench Pro and Sustains 8-Hour Autonomous Execution
articles.summary: Z.AI released GLM-5.1, a 754B open-weight agentic model achieving state-of-the-art on SWE-Bench Pro with advanced coding capabilities and 8-hour autonomous execution support.

articles.title: How to Combine Google Search, Google Maps, and Custom Functions in a Single Gemini API Call With Context Circulation, Parallel Tool IDs, and Multi-Step Agentic Chains
articles.summary: Tutorial demonstrates combining Google Search, Maps, and custom functions in single Gemini API calls using context circulation and parallel tool IDs for multi-step agentic chains with real-time location data.

articles.title: How to Deploy Open WebUI with Secure OpenAI API Integration, Public Tunneling, and Browser-Based Chat Access
articles.summary: This tutorial demonstrates deploying Open WebUI in Google Colab with secure OpenAI API integration, environment configuration, and public tunnel access for browser-based chat functionality.

articles.title: How I sync my smart thermostat with off-peak hour rates to reduce electricity costs
articles.summary: User automates smart thermostat with IFTTT to align temperature settings with off-peak electricity rates, reducing energy costs while maintaining comfort.

articles.title: I tested the Suunto Spark earbuds for a month: Why air conduction audio is a winner
articles.summary: Suunto's new air conduction earbuds outperform bone conduction headphones for workouts, offering superior audio quality. The tester switched from bone conduction to these earbuds for better sound experience.

articles.title: I tested a portable wind turbine for 2 years: Why it's my backup, not a solar replacement
articles.summary: After two years of testing, portable wind turbines prove less practical than solar energy due to requiring constant maintenance and management to maintain efficiency.

articles.title: A Hands-On Guide to Testing Agents with RAGAs and G-Eval
articles.summary: A practical guide for testing AI agents using RAGAS and G-Eval evaluation frameworks. These tools help assess agent performance and quality in retrieval-augmented generation systems.

articles.title: Handling Race Conditions in Multi-Agent Orchestration
articles.summary: Race conditions occur when multiple agents simultaneously write to the same resource, producing corrupted or nonsensical results. The article addresses handling these conflicts in multi-agent orchestration systems.

articles.title: Top 5 Reranking Models to Improve RAG Results
articles.summary: The article discusses top reranking models that enhance retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems by improving result quality and relevance.

articles.title: Detecting Translation Hallucinations with Attention Misalignment
articles.summary: Researchers present a low-budget method for detecting translation hallucinations in neural machine translation systems through attention misalignment analysis, enabling token-level uncertainty estimation.

articles.title: How to Use Claude Code to Build a Minimum Viable Product
articles.summary: Learn how to build minimum viable products using Claude Code's coding agents to effectively present product ideas efficiently.

articles.title: Grounding Your LLM: A Practical Guide to RAG for Enterprise Knowledge Bases
articles.summary: This guide provides practical foundations for implementing Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to ground large language models using enterprise knowledge bases.

articles.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
articles.summary: NVIDIA highlights physical AI breakthroughs during National Robotics Week, showcasing how robots transform industries. Advances in robot learning, simulation, and foundation models accelerate real-world deployment from virtual training.

articles.title: From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI
articles.summary: Google and NVIDIA optimized Gemma 4 models for local on-device AI execution across various systems, from data centers to RTX PCs and the DGX Spark supercomputer. These small, efficient models enable real-time contextual insights without relying on cloud infrastructure.

articles.title: Press Start on April: GeForce NOW Brings 10 Games to the Cloud
articles.summary: GeForce NOW launches 10 new games in April, including Capcom's PRAGMATA and Arknights: Endfield, a new 3D real-time strategy game.

articles.title: Manage AI costs with Amazon Bedrock Projects
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock Projects enables organizations to track and attribute AI inference costs to specific workloads, allowing cost analysis through AWS Cost Explorer and Data Exports for optimization and chargeback purposes.

articles.title: Building real-time conversational podcasts with Amazon Nova 2 Sonic
articles.summary: Amazon Nova 2 Sonic enables creators to produce high-quality podcasts at scale with natural conversational AI, reducing traditional production time and costs while supporting multiple languages.

articles.title: Text-to-SQL solution powered by Amazon Bedrock
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock's text-to-SQL solution enables business users to self-serve data queries, reducing bottlenecks and freeing technical teams for complex work.

articles.title: Integrate Physical AI Capabilities into Existing Apps with NVIDIA Omniverse Libraries
articles.summary: NVIDIA Omniverse Libraries enable developers to integrate Physical AI capabilities into existing applications, allowing teams to design and validate robots in simulated environments before deployment.

articles.title: Running AI Workloads on Rack-Scale Supercomputers: From Hardware to Topology-Aware Scheduling
articles.summary: NVIDIA's GB200 and GB300 NVL72 systems are rack-scale supercomputers using Blackwell architecture with 18 compute trays and high-bandwidth networking for AI workloads.

articles.title: Accelerating Vision AI Pipelines with Batch Mode VC-6 and NVIDIA Nsight
articles.summary: NVIDIA accelerates vision AI pipelines using batch mode VC-6 and Nsight tools to improve throughput in decode, preprocessing, and GPU scheduling stages.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study with CrowdDNA examines how different generations perceive and respond to social media advertisements across various platforms.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta aims for net zero emissions by 2030 through developing carbon mitigation technology and sharing it openly.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: A person with an unconventional background successfully developed innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse perspectives can drive breakthrough advancements in emerging tech fields.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers develop methods for understanding LLM behavior at scale through feature attribution and interaction analysis, advancing AI interpretability and trustworthiness. The work aims to make complex model decision-making more transparent to developers and stakeholders.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on designing imaging systems using information-driven approaches. An encoder maps objects to images which are then corrupted by noise, with an information estimator quantifying measurement quality.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new RL algorithm uses divide-and-conquer instead of temporal difference learning, avoiding TD's scalability issues and enabling better performance on long-horizon tasks.
